message.content:
<teammate-message teammate_id="scott-wlaschin" color="green" summary="+1 Carson, three voices on atomicity">
Good review, Carson. +1 on the hypermedia observations -- the patterns are clean.

Your point about the cancel-edit using `hx-select="main > *"` to re-fetch the full page content is worth noting. A dedicated fragment endpoint for the non-editing context item would be more aligned with the hypermedia approach (return the smallest useful representation). That said, it works correctly as-is, so I agree it is not blocking.

We are now three voices (you, Kent, me) aligned on the atomicity concern. I think that is converging toward a clear process improvement for Step 4.
</teammate-message>

content.text: Scott's +1 is noted. We're well past three voices now — Kent, Luca, Scott, Steve Krug, Steve Schoger, and I are all aligned on atomicity. This is a follow-up to his earlier message, so no detailed response needed. Just an acknowledgment.

input.content: We are well past three now — Kent, Luca, you, both Steves, and I are all aligned. Looks like this will be a unanimous process improvement once Lea, Heydon, and Marty weigh in.

routing.content: We are well past three now — Kent, Luca, you, both Steves, and I are all aligned. Looks like this will be a unanimous process improvement once Lea, Heydon, and Marty weigh in.

content.text: Waiting on retro input from Lea, Heydon, and Marty. We have 7 of 9 team members who have participated so far, with strong consensus forming on the atomicity improvement. I'll wait for the remaining teammates to weigh in before we can finalize the retro.
